levelledPara.title: General
levelledPara.para: This data module gives the redundancy and degraded mode description for the Hybrid Electric Architecture Overview applicable to the AMPEL360E Wide Tube-and-Wing Family baseline.

The module describes the top-level redundancy philosophy, degraded operation principles, fault containment boundaries, availability management, safe-state transition logic, maintenance reporting context, and traceability context associated with ATLAS 070-000 / 00A.

levelledPara.title: Redundancy and Degraded Mode Scope
levelledPara.para: The scope includes redundancy and degraded operation at architecture level for energy source interfaces, energy conversion equipment, electrical distribution paths, propulsion drive equipment, thermal management functions, control and monitoring functions, protection functions, and maintenance reporting interfaces.

This data module is descriptive only. It does not define final system safety probabilities, dispatch categories, minimum equipment list provisions, certified failure thresholds, equipment qualification values, or detailed recovery procedures. Those items shall be controlled by the applicable safety, certification, operations, engineering, and maintenance baselines.

levelledPara.title: Redundancy Philosophy
levelledPara.para: The redundancy philosophy is intended to maintain controlled hybrid-electric system behaviour after single equipment faults, partial loss of function, degraded monitoring, reduced thermal capacity, or reduced energy availability.
Redundancy shall prevent uncontrolled propagation of faults across energy, electrical, propulsion drive, thermal, control, monitoring, and maintenance reporting boundaries.

levelledPara.title: Principal Redundancy Areas
levelledPara.para: The hybrid-electric architecture may include redundancy provisions in the following areas:

listItem.para: Energy source availability and isolation monitoring.
Propulsion drive channels and local protection functions.
Thermal management and heat rejection capacity.
Control, monitoring, sensor, and communication functions.
Maintenance reporting and diagnostic data paths.

levelledPara.title: Energy Source Redundancy
levelledPara.para: Energy source redundancy addresses the ability of the architecture to detect reduced source availability, isolate an affected source path, inhibit unavailable energy paths, and maintain controlled system behaviour with the remaining approved energy availability.
Energy source redundancy does not imply continued full propulsion performance after source degradation unless explicitly authorized by the certified programme configuration.

levelledPara.title: Energy Conversion Redundancy
levelledPara.para: Energy conversion redundancy addresses the ability to detect loss, limitation, or degradation of conversion equipment and to maintain a controlled operating condition through alternate conversion capacity, load limitation, equipment isolation, or safe-state transition.
Conversion degradation shall be reported to the applicable control, indication, recording, diagnostic, and maintenance reporting functions.

levelledPara.title: Electrical Distribution Redundancy
levelledPara.para: Electrical distribution redundancy addresses controlled routing, switching, protection, and isolation of electrical power paths after a detected abnormal condition.

Distribution redundancy may include alternate feeds, segmented power paths, independent protection zones, contactor state monitoring, isolation logic, and fault containment boundaries as defined by the approved architecture baseline.

levelledPara.title: Propulsion Drive Redundancy
levelledPara.para: Propulsion drive redundancy addresses the ability of the architecture to maintain controlled propulsive behaviour after degradation of drive electronics, electric machines, local sensors, local protection functions, or drive communication paths.
Drive degradation may cause power limitation, channel isolation, increased monitoring, maintenance message generation, dispatch limitation, or transition to degraded or safe-state mode.

levelledPara.title: Thermal Management Redundancy
levelledPara.para: Thermal management redundancy addresses the ability to maintain equipment temperatures within controlled limits after reduced cooling capacity, degraded heat transfer, reduced heat rejection, sensor degradation, or loss of a thermal control function.
Reduced thermal redundancy may require propulsion energy limitation, increased thermal monitoring, load reduction, degraded-mode operation, or safe-state transition.

levelledPara.title: Control and Monitoring Redundancy
levelledPara.para: Control and monitoring redundancy addresses the availability of valid command paths, sensor data, operating state data, protection status, communication paths, and diagnostic records required to maintain deterministic system behaviour.
Loss of monitoring confidence shall be treated as a degradation when the system can no longer confirm required operating limits, isolation states, protection states, or equipment availability.

levelledPara.title: Protection Redundancy
levelledPara.para: Protection redundancy addresses the ability to detect, isolate, limit, or shut down abnormal energy, electrical, thermal, propulsion drive, control, or communication conditions.

Protection functions shall support fault containment, safe-state transition, inhibited restart, maintenance message generation, and retained diagnostic evidence as required by the approved safety baseline.

levelledPara.title: Degraded Mode Philosophy
levelledPara.para: Degraded mode is a controlled operating condition entered after partial loss of function, reduced redundancy, reduced energy availability, reduced thermal capacity, equipment limitation, monitoring degradation, or detected abnormal condition.

Degraded mode shall maintain deterministic behaviour, prevent uncontrolled fault propagation, and make the reduced system state available to crew indication, recording, maintenance, and diagnostic functions.

levelledPara.title: Degraded Mode Entry Conditions
levelledPara.para: Degraded mode may be entered after detection of one or more of the following conditions:

listItem.para: Loss or limitation of an energy source path.
Loss or limitation of energy conversion equipment.
Electrical distribution fault or protection trip.
Sensor, control, monitoring, or communication degradation.
Detected abnormal condition requiring controlled limitation.

levelledPara.title: Degraded Mode Behaviour
levelledPara.para: In degraded mode, the architecture may continue operation with reduced power, reduced propulsion availability, restricted energy paths, reduced thermal capacity, increased monitoring, limited control authority, or inhibited operating modes.
Degraded mode behaviour shall remain predictable, bounded, traceable, and consistent with the approved safety assessment and certification baseline.

levelledPara.title: Fault Containment Boundaries
levelledPara.para: Fault containment may include electrical isolation, load limitation, channel shutdown, thermal limitation, command inhibition, safe-state transition, maintenance reporting, and retained fault recording.

levelledPara.title: Load Shedding and Power Limitation
levelledPara.para: Load shedding and power limitation functions may reduce propulsion energy demand, auxiliary load demand, or equipment operating level to maintain system operation within safe and controlled limits.
Load shedding priorities, power limits, recovery criteria, and crew indication requirements shall be defined by the approved system control, safety, operations, and certification baselines.

levelledPara.title: Safe-State Transition
levelledPara.para: Safe-state transition is commanded when the architecture can no longer maintain controlled operation within the approved degraded operating envelope.

Safe-state transition may include energy path isolation, propulsion drive inhibition, protective shutdown, retained protection trip status, inhibited restart, maintenance message generation, and diagnostic data retention.

levelledPara.title: Recovery from Degraded Mode
levelledPara.para: Recovery from degraded mode shall occur only when the applicable readiness checks, reset conditions, inspection results, maintenance actions, or ground support checks have satisfied the approved recovery criteria.

Automatic recovery, crew-commanded recovery, maintenance-commanded recovery, and ground-support recovery shall be separately controlled by the approved safety, control, operations, and maintenance baselines.

levelledPara.title: Crew Indication Context
levelledPara.para: Redundancy loss, degraded function, limited operation, inhibited transition, safe-state entry, and recovery status shall be made available to the applicable crew indication and recording functions as required by the approved aircraft configuration.
Detailed alerting logic, annunciation text, crew action logic, and operational procedures are outside the scope of this data module.

levelledPara.title: Maintenance Reporting Context
levelledPara.para: Redundancy loss, degraded operation, protection events, failed readiness checks, inhibited transitions, safe-state entries, and recovery attempts shall generate maintenance or diagnostic records as required by the approved maintenance reporting baseline.
Fault isolation logic, corrective action references, dispatch effects, and return-to-service checks shall be specified in the applicable fault isolation and maintenance data modules.

levelledPara.title: Redundancy and Degraded Mode Boundaries
levelledPara.para: This data module describes redundancy and degraded mode principles at architecture level only. It does not allocate certified redundancy levels, final failure probabilities, dispatch categories, time limits, equipment ratings, annunciation text, or maintenance task intervals.
Those values shall be defined in controlled engineering datasets, safety assessment records, operational documentation, maintenance procedures, verification plans, and certification evidence.

levelledPara.title: Traceability Context
levelledPara.para: This data module supports deterministic traceability between architecture-level redundancy definition, degraded-mode logic, protection logic, S1000D CSDB descriptive content, maintenance reporting, safety assessment evidence, verification evidence, and lifecycle governance records.

levelledPara.title: Authoring Status
levelledPara.para: This issue is an unverified scaffold. The content shall be completed, reviewed, and validated against the applicable BREX data module, business rules, applicability model, terminology rules, redundancy baseline, degraded-mode baseline, control baseline, interface control baseline, programme configuration baseline, safety assessment evidence, and verification records before CSDB release.
